items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 24 at 1:10PM AKDT until April 25 at 4:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South winds 25 to 35 mph with gusts up to 60 mph.

* WHERE...Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp.

* WHEN...Until 4 AM AKDT Saturday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.areas: Eastern Alaska Range North of Trims Camp
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: Wind Advisory issued April 24 at 1:10PM AKDT until April 25 at 4:00AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Southeast winds 20 to 30 mph with gusts up to 50 mph
expected.

* WHERE...Delta Junction.

* WHEN...Until 4 AM AKDT Saturday.

* IMPACTS...Gusty winds will blow around unsecured objects. Tree
limbs could be blown down and a few power outages may result.
items.instruction:
Winds this strong can make driving difficult, especially for high
profile vehicles. Use extra caution.

items.headline: Special Weather Statement issued April 24 at 11:51AM AKDT by NWS Fairbanks AK
items.description:
Temperatures across portions of the Yukon Flats remain on the
cooler side, allowing them to drop below freezing during the
overnight hours. A system will be moving across the White
Mountains early Saturday morning, bringing a chance for a glaze of
ice across isolated areas. The best chance for a light glaze of
ice will be from 3AM AKST to 10AM AKST.

For the latest forecast information visit our website,
weather.gov/afg.

items.headline: High Wind Watch issued April 24 at 11:14AM AKDT until April 26 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...South to southwest winds 35 to 55 mph with gusts up to 70
to 80 mph possible.

* WHERE...Unalaska and Nikolski.

* WHEN...From Saturday evening through Sunday afternoon.

* IMPACTS...High winds could move loose debris, damage property, and
cause power outages. Travel could be difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...The highest forecast wind speed will depend
on the track of a low in the Bering Sea, which is uncertain at
this time. If the low tracks further east, gusts as high as 70-80
mph ar
items.instruction:
A high wind watch means there is potential for a hazardous high wind
event. Monitor the latest forecasts and warnings for updates on this
situation. Fasten loose objects or shelter objects in a safe
location prior to the onset of winds.

items.headline: High Wind Warning issued April 24 at 11:14AM AKDT until April 26 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
* WHAT...Southwest winds 45 to 60 mph with gusts up to 80 mph
expected.

* WHERE...Atka and Adak.

* WHEN...From 7 PM Saturday to 4 PM AKDT Sunday.

* IMPACTS...High winds may move loose debris, damage property, and
cause power outages. Travel will be difficult.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Strong easterly winds move in Saturday
morning, but the highest winds and worst conditions will begin
when winds turn southerly Saturday evening. There may be a period
of weaker winds Saturday night as the storm c
items.instruction:
People are urged to secure loose objects that could be blown around
or damaged by the wind. Prepare for the possibility of widespread
power outages.

items.headline: Avalanche Warning issued April 24 at 7:51AM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
items.description:
SABAFC

The following message is transmitted at the request of the
Valdez Avalanche Center.

...THE VALDEZ AVALANCHE CENTER HAS ISSUED AN AVALANCHE WARNING...

* WHAT... Wet heavy snow and strong southeasterly winds are
overloading a weak snowpack. Very dangerous avalanche conditions
exist.

* WHERE...For the mountains surrounding the Valdez and Thompson
Pass area, including Ernestine.

* WHEN...In effect from 8AM FRIDAY (TODAY) THROUGH 8AM SATURDAY
AKST.
